item.title: 美国军方称袭击了伊朗 10 个目标，因袭击第二天导致停火紧张
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military said Saturday it had struck 10 targets in Iran at PresidentDonald Trump’sdirection, continuing a string of attacks that have shakenthe war’suneasy ceasefire.
U.S. Central Command, in a post to social media, said that U.S. military aircraft targeted Iranian military “surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone storage facilities, and minelayer capabilities” following an attack on a merchant vessel early on Saturday morning. It later specified the strikes involved 10 Iranian military targets at multiple locations in and near the Strait of Hormuz.
The ongoing strikes in the Persian Gulf show the danger of the Iran war again spinning out of control, even after Iran and the U.S. reachedan interim deal to try and agree on a final accord to end the conflict.
In a social media post, Trump said the U.S. had “struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations, and coastal radar sites, for violating the Cease Fire Agreement, AGAIN!” He warned of a point where the U.S. may no longer be able to be reasonable “and will be forced to militarily complete the job.”
“If that happens, the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist!” Trump wrote on Truth Social.
The incident follows a similar back and forth that occurred just days prior whenan Iranian drone struck a merchant vesseloff the coast of Oman on Thursday and the U.S. military retaliated with strikes the next day.
U.S. Central Command said that in this latest attack Iranian forces attacked the oil tanker Kiku with a one-way drone. The tanker was laden with more than two million barrels of crude oil and sailing through the Strait of Hormuz.
According to ship tracking websites, the Kiku left a Qatari oil field in the middle of the Persian Gulf earlier in the week and was bound for a port in the United Arab Emirates that sits on the Gulf of Aman, just on the other side of the Strait of Hormuz.
It appeared to be attempting to use a route that was established near the coast of Oman that is serving as an alternative to the route sanctioned by Iran that runs through its own waters.
A multinational maritime body overseen by the U.S. Navy said Saturday that it would expand the Omani route to allow for both inbound and outbound traffic, likely setting up a new flashpoint with Tehran, which sees the strait as a key source of leverage inongoing talkswith the U.S.
The U.S. military said that “Iran had a chance to honor the ceasefire agreement” but “elected not to” when its forces attacked the Kiku.
Iran state TV reported explosions in an area just north of the Strait of Hormuz.
Earlier on Saturday, a statement from Bahrain’s Foreign Ministry said a “number of Iranian drones” targeted the country. It called the attack “a flagrant threat to the security of citizens and residents.” There were no immediate reports of damage.
Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard earlier issued a statement carried by the state-run IRNA news agency saying it had targeted several locations “of the U.S. terrorist army in the region.” It did not name what areas were targeted.
Bahrain has been one of the strongest critics of Iran and is home to the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet. It just hosted U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio fora meeting of the Gulf Cooperation Council’s foreign ministers, which ended with a call for an end to Iran’s attacks and for the strait to be completely open.
Overnight into Saturday, the U.S. military’s Central Command said it had struck Iranian missile and drone locations and coastal radar sites.
U.S. Vice President JD Vance, who has led the negotiations with Iran, said on social media Friday night that Iran should “pick up the phone” if there are disagreements about the ceasefire agreement, “but violence will be met with violence.”
The U.S. and Iran are negotiating terms of the deal including issues such as getting ships through the strait that’s vital to global supplies of oil and natural gas and addressing the future ofIran’s nuclear program and stockpile of highly enriched uranium.
Under the interim deal, the two sides have 60 days to work out the details. Ending thefighting in Lebanonbetween Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group is a key part of the deal.
The British military’s United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said that a tanker was attacked Saturday in the strait, with the crew safe and no environmental damage reported. No one immediately claimed the strike, but suspicion fell on Iran.
Just after that report, the Joint Maritime Information Center, overseen by the U.S. Navy, said the route near Oman’s shore is expanding to allow for inbound and outbound traffic.
Iran has insisted that ships must obey its orders and warned it will start charging fees for transit through the strait. However, ships have been increasingly trying to leave the Gulf in recent days.
Ebrahim Azizi, who heads the Iranian parliament’s national security commission, wrote Friday that “the Strait of Hormuz is governed by Iran, so: Respect the rules.”
The U.S. and Gulf Arab states have rejected Iran’s demands. The strait is considered as an international waterway, despite being the territorial waters of Iran and Oman.
The Joint Maritime Information Center warned that the threat to ships was “substantial,” adding that “mariners are advised of the existence of mines and should expect a naval presence as clearance operations continue.”
The International Maritime Organization on Fridayhalted a new effort to evacuate shipssaid it won’t resume until there are guarantees that the other ships won’t be attacked. It said about 115 ships have been able to move out of the strait in recent days.
Toropin reported from Washington, Associated Press writer Ali Swenson in New York contributed to this report.

item.title: 地震死亡人数达到 1,430 人，委内瑞拉民众的沮丧情绪与日俱增
item.description:
LA GUAIRA, Venezuela (AP) — Tensions flared Saturday as desperation grew in Venezuela’s state of La Guaira as rescuers and civilians searched for earthquake survivors and the death toll rose sharply to 1,430.
Families reported at least 68,900 people missing Saturday, three days after theone-two punch of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakesdevastated the South American nation.
Venezuelans looking for loved ones and neighborsused shovels, heavy equipment, ropes and bare hands atop mounds of toppled concrete throughout La Guaira, one of the hardest-hit states. They were joined by a growing number of international rescue teams who began to climb through the rubble, offering a small glint of hope to anguished families.
Tensions peaked over what many Venezuelans viewed as an inadequate response by the government, whose soldiers, firefighters, police and military cadets were evidently underprepared to respond to the scope of the tragedy. Frustration was amplified by efforts to project the image of a robust state response.
Aid agencies consider the first 48 to 72 hours as crucial for retrieving people alive, though that can be extended if they have access to food and water. Venezuelan officials said 17 flights carrying more than 1,600 rescue team members had touched down by Saturday.
As 72 hours passed since the earthquakes struck, many felt every minute ticking away as they ran out of time to rescue people alive.
“There’s a pile of bodies over there from last night. Newborn babies,” said Mileidy Romero, who was among those searching in the seaside town of Caraballeada. “At 8 p.m. (yesterday) there were people alive down there, and they haven’t bothered to rescue them. We’ve located several bodies, and they haven’t helped us recover them either. What are they waiting for?”
Damaged buildings are seen three days after earthquakes struck in La Guaira, Venezuela, Saturday, June 27, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)
Acting President Delcy Rodríguez said on state television that more than 14,000 members of the military and police are patrolling the area, where access is now blocked and special permits are required to enter. But many in disaster zones said they had seen little of their government.
Some people climbed the remnants of buildings and cried out names, hoping for any proof of life. Dust coated coastal communities. In punishing heat, more people wore masks as the stench of decomposition spread. In other parts of La Guaira, teams loaded stacks of bodies – some in white bags, other naked – onto white trucks from the ground of a dirt hospital parking lot, where they were being identified.
People camp outside their apartment building three days after the earthquakes damaged their homes in La Guiara Venezuela, Saturday, June 27, 2026.. (AP Photo/Fernando Vergara)
Without hard hats or other gear, rescuers and civilians instead wore motorcycle helmets as they searched piles of debris that were once people’s belongings: Eddie Murphy and Nemo DVDs, a kitchen sink, mattresses and shoes.
Some, frustrated by the government’s response, blocked an excavator from leaving the site of a collapse and pulled the operator from its cabin shortly after state workers took selfies in front of flattened buildings and left without helping. The ruling party’s officials often take selfies to show participation in government-related events.
A few feet away, at least five bodies wrapped in blankets.
A member of the crowd, Yeison Marcano, said those searching had received some assistance from an investigations unit but neither police nor the National Guard helped.
“They came to eat arepas and take pictures to make it look like they were working,” Marcano said. “They didn’t even get their uniforms dirty like we have. We’ve been here for three days.”
A minute later, a man tried to grab a firefighter, shouting and cursing. “Silence! Silence!” rescuers shouted as they tried to confirm whether someone was trapped alive.
Meanwhile, an older man was pulled from the rubble of a public housing building. Visibly disoriented, he begged a nurse for water. He fought with personnel who put him into a pickup truck, screaming, “My family! My family!”
The International Organization for Migration said over 6 million people could be affected, some 2 million in the capital, Caracas, alone.
Experts said the destruction was amplified by the quick succession of shallow quakes. For days, smaller aftershocks occasionally shook the capital, Caracas and areas hit by the quakes, including one measuring 4.8 on Saturday.
The disaster poses a huge challenge forRodríguez, the former vice president who took office in January after the U.S. capture and removal of then-PresidentNicolás Maduro. Venezuela has been facingeconomic disarrayfor more than a decade, and many people reject the legitimacy ofthe political movement Rodríguez represents.
Search teams and foreign aid continued to arrive from Mexico, the U.S., Brazil, El Salvador, France and elsewhere.
On Saturday, Mexican rescue teams climbed over collapsed buildings and pushed their heads into holes in thepancaked concreteto search for signs of life, occasionally hearing movement.
“We’re rescuers from the Mexican military, if there’s anyone down there still alive, make noise or scream. Now!” one man shouted.
For many, the images of international aid teams arriving and climbing through the rubble alongside them offered a glimmer of hope. Yonahí Regalado has been calling out the names of her sister and 1-year-old nephew and godson since 1 a.m. the day after the earthquakes until aid workers began to arrive.
“It doesn’t matter who it is, whoever, whether it’s family or somebody else. If there is anyone alive, let’s get them out,” she said, as helicopters circled overhead.
Small moments of humanity mixed with grief and terror.
A woman cries next to damaged buildings three days after an earthquake struck in La Guaira, Venezuela, Saturday, June 27, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)
Rescue teams carefully handed down a 18-day-old swaddled in pink blankets from a building after 12 hours of searching for the baby boy and his mother, Telemundo reported. One 69-year-old woman, saved by Salvadoran teams, asked for a Coca-Cola upon being pulled out of the rubble, Salvadoran authorities said.
One video showed a Venezuelan rescuer comforting an elderly woman trapped beneath the rubble, scared that the structure would cave in if she moved.
“The roof won’t cave in. If it falls, I’ll be here with you,” he said.
Simón Bolívar International Airport, which serves Caracas, was badly damaged. One runway was operational as U.S. teams worked to repair the crucial throughway, Jeremy Lewin, a senior State Department official in charge of foreign assistance, told reporters.
Lewin said a U.S. Navy transport ship was docked off the coast, ready to receive airlifted survivors in need of medical attention. Lewin said it is a “race against the clock” to find people injured in the quakes.
Residents and rescue workers search through the rubble two days after an earthquake struck in La Guaira, Venezuela, Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)
Janetsky reported from Mexico City. Associated Press journalists Juan Pablo Arraez and Matías Delacroix in La Guaira, Venezuela, Clara Preve in Buenos Aires, Argentina, and Ali Swenson in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: 瑞士、丹麦和捷克共和国打破高温记录，中欧热火朝天
item.description:
BERLIN (AP) — Temperatures soared to record highs from Switzerland to the Czech Republic and Denmark on Saturday, as aheat wave that baked western European countriesthis week moved to central and eastern parts of the continent.
Unusually high temperatures were recorded even in the Nordic countries not known for sweltering summers. Denmark’s Meteorological Institute reported a record 37 degrees Celsius (98.6 degrees Fahrenheit) in Ødum north of Aarhus — the warmest day since records there began in 1874.
In Switzerland, a record 38.8 C (101.8 F) was set in the city of Basel.
Germany’s famous Autobahn was overwhelmed, too, as temperatures were expected to hit 40 C (104 F). In two places outside Berlin, the concrete of the A2 burst due to the high temperatures and the highway had to be closed. Other highway damage was reported across the country, according to the German daily Bild.
Train operator Deutsche Bahn and other rail companies advised against all nonessential train travel this weekend.
“Germany’s transportation infrastructure is being severely affected by the record-breaking heat this weekend,” Deutsche Bahn said in a statement.
The Czech Republic also saw its hottest day on record, with 40.8 C (105.4 F) in the northern town of Doksany. Forecasters said it may still rise.
In the western German city of Dormagen, dozens of residents of a nursing home were evacuated for medical care due to dangerous heat conditions in the building.
The local fire department reported that temperatures inside the home had reached 35 C (95 F). Air conditioning is not widespread in Germany and many countries in Europe because the continent is largely unused to such oppressive heat.
A resident at the home died overnight, but it was not yet clear whether the heat was the cause, a city spokesperson told German news agency dpa.
In France, multiple towns in the east of the country saw their highest-ever temperatures Saturday, with some above 40 C (104 F) even though the worst of the heat wave was starting to pass in some regions.
Paris and 36 other regions, stretching from the center to the east and northeast, remained in the extreme-heat red zone on Saturday, down from a peak on Thursday of 72 regions that were under such warnings. The capital continued to see unrelenting pressure on its hospitals, with a second consecutive day of nearly 3,000 people seeking care in public hospital emergency rooms, about a third more than normal.
The Paris public hospital authority, AP-HP, said it activated its emergency response plan across all 38 hospitals to cope. Phone calls to its medical dispatch centers were up nearly 80% compared with the same period in 2025, it said.
Concerns that hospitals could be overwhelmed prompted the postponement of the Paris Pride march for LGBTQ+ rights on Saturday, and a three-day music festival was canceled.
The temperatures this week have been higher than those during a historic 2003 heat wave that was blamed for 15,000 heat-related deaths, many of them older people. The AP-HP’s director, Nicolas Revel, said he doesn’t expect as many deaths this time, at least in Paris hospitals, in part because treatment for overheating has since improved.
During another exceptionally hot summer last year, more than 5,700 deaths were attributed to heat, according to France’s public health authority.
“I think we’ll be situated, clearly, between 2025 and without necessarily reaching the catastrophic level of 2003. But we have to expect that there will still be many deaths,” he said.
In the U.K., sweltering conditions are expected to gradually ease this weekend though an amber warning — one step down from red — remained in place until Saturday night.
Britons struggled to cope this week as the record June temperature was smashed three days in a row. Friday was confirmed as the country’s hottest June day on record, with a provisional temperature of 37.3 C (99 F) recorded in eastern England.
It was more than 1 C hotter than the long-standing record for June heat in the U.K., set in the summer of 1976.
On Saturday, police said the bodies of a 22-year-old man and a 15-year-old boy were recovered from a lake and a river. The deaths bring the total number of U.K. heat-related fatalities this week to four.
Authorities in the U.K. have warned people to take extra care when swimming in unsupervised areas following the deaths of around 40 people in France over the past week.
In Italy’s capital, which remains under a red heat alert, tourists tried to cool off seeking shade near buildings and dunking their heads under public fountains. Street vendors were doing a brisk business selling bottled water, hats and sun umbrellas.
Some turned to Italian classics for relief.
“Gelato, pasta, because it’s tradition, but also fresh fruit, and ice cold drinks, that’s the best for this temperature,” said Isabella Dold, a tourist from Kempten, Germany.
On Saturday, Italy’s health ministry said 18 cities — including the most popular tourism hubs like Venice, Florence, Bologna and Milan — were on red alert due to danger posed by the high temperatures.
Anew studyfrom the World Weather Attribution, a Europe-based collaboration of scientists, reported Friday that the record-breaking heat and humidity in Europe this week would not have been possiblewithout climate change.
The rapid study found that the heat would have been virtually impossible just five decades ago, and is 200 times more likely today than it would have been 20 years ago.
André Corrêa do Lago, the president of the U.N. climate talks known as COP30, said the heat wave has “helped strengthen the perception of urgency of fighting climate change.”
“The fact that we are living with this amazing heat in London is a strong argument, we need to agree, that we have to take action as soon as possible,” do Lago told The Associated Press.
Hui reported from London and Leicester from Paris. Associated Press journalists Trisha Thomas in Rome, Suman Naishadham in Madrid and Karel Janicek in Prague contributed to this report.

item.title: 美国最大的犹他州大火烧毁森林，大风阻碍了救援人员的工作
item.description:
MARYSVALE, Utah (AP) — The largest wildfire in the United States marched through canyons and over mountainsides Saturday, blackening an already parched landscape in Utah as residents on the flanks of the blaze watched smoke billowing when the winds picked up.
TheCottonwood Fire, burning in rugged terrain in southwest Utah, severely damaged the Eagle Point ski resort and destroyed summer cabins in sparsely populated Beaver County. Authorities conducted damage assessments but did not yet have estimates of how many homes burned.
A plume of smoke rises from the Cottonwood Fire, Friday, June 26, 2026, near Beaver, Utah. (AP Photo/Ty ONeil)
Gov. Spencer Cox, in a social media post, asked for prayers for firefighters and “the rains we desperately need.”
“It’s as bleak as it’s ever been ... and yet there were several miraculous stops and saves,” Cox said, referring to the work of the hundreds of firefighters assigned to the blaze.
They were among the crews that have poured into Utah as more wildfires in the arid state gained steam thanks to consecutive days of what forecasters called critical fire weather, or dangerously low humidity levels, warm temperatures and gusty winds.
There was little relief from the strong winds Saturday, but crews fighting the Cottonwood Fire were treated to higher humidity levels than the single digits that were expected, said Alyssa Mason, a spokesperson assigned to the blaze.
“That was good news for us today,” Mason said.
Sunrise on the Cottonwood Fire in Kingston, Utah, on Saturday , June 27, 2026. (AP Photo/Ty ONeil)
Utah is coming off a record-low snowpack and the warmest winter on record, which has elevated fire danger. Much of the West is grappling with similar conditions, according tothe National Interagency Fire Center.
From Alaska to Florida, crews worked to corral dozens of fires, including three dozen that were classified as large and uncontained.
Nationally, nearly 3 million acres (1.2 million hectares) have burned since the start of the year, pushing the U.S. ahead of the 10-year average.
Helicopters took to the skies hoping to put a dent in the Cottonwood Fire before afternoon winds kicked up again and grounded them. Air support also was grounded the previous afternoon as conditions deteriorated.
The fire ballooned to more than 144 square miles (373 square kilometers), and roughly 1,300 residents in the towns of Marysvale, Junction and Circleville remained on notice that they should be prepared to leave if conditions worsened.
Cox declared an emergency, and the state has restricted fireworks citing one of the most severe wildfire seasons in recent history and saying the blazes were stretching wildland firefighting capabilities.
“These fires are starting closer to homes and communities,” State Forester Jamie Barnes said. “One human-caused fire is one too many.”
The cause of the Cottonwood Fire has not been determined, but officials said the majority of those sparked so far this year have been human-caused. Forecasters also noted that lightning strikes around the West have resulted in fires.
Elsewhere in Utah, evacuations were in place for several small communities southwest of Salt Lake City, including Eureka, with a population under 1,000, and the Vernon Reservoir area. Highways running through the area were closed.
Two wildfires in that area — the Iron and the Cherry — covered a combined 112 square miles (290 square kilometers), fire officials said.
The towering column of smoke from the Cottonwood Fire was visible Friday from miles away, even from Bryce Canyon National Park to the south and Colorado to the east. It was hazy Saturday as the smoke drifted to the northeast, leaving air quality unaffected in places like Bryce and other popular vacation spots to the south.
Bob Miller of Yucaipa, California, who was staying at an RV park in Marysvale, was prepared to evacuate if needed. The evenings especially have been smoky, Miller said, but he and his family have still been able to enjoy the area, where they have vacationed for almost two decades.
“Fires happen around here. When you come every year, you kind of get used to that,” he added.
“After you’ve been through burned areas a few times, you look at it and you understand what nature can do.”
Firefighters gather near the Cottonwood Fire near Junction, Utah, on Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Ty ONeil)
The warnings stretched from California to Arizona to New Mexico, where firefighters battled several blazes amid windy conditions. That included a new one reported south of Grand Canyon National Park.
Authorities said the flames were moving away from Grand Canyon Village and the nearby community of Tusayan. The area was without power as the utility serving the area initiated a safety shut-off earlier in hopes of lessening the wildfire risk.
Park visitors could still purchase park passes at entrance stations as long as backup power systems remained operational, but officials said people should come prepared. That meant downloading maps before arriving and ensuring that phones and other devices are fully charged.
The sun sets over the Cottonwood Fire near Marysvale, Utah, on Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Ty ONeil)
Power shut-offs have become more common in the West as wildfire risk has expanded. It is usually a last resort after utility forecasters weigh factors like sustained wind and gust speeds, available fuels and topography.
With extreme fire conditions persisting in Utah, Rocky Mountain Power also shut off power lines serving Beaver County and other areas.
Montoya Bryan reported from Albuquerque, New Mexico. Associated Press writers Jamie Stengle in Dallas; Julie Carr Smyth in Columbus, Ohio; and Ed White in Detroit contributed.

item.title: 亚利桑那州高速公路拖车中发现女孩后，一夫多妻教派领导人被判虐待罪
item.description:
菲尼克斯（美联社）——周五，一名一夫多妻教派领导人因策划涉及儿童的性行为而被判 50 年联邦监狱刑，他在亚利桑那州拖着一辆不通风的拖车时，在一辆不通风的拖车里发现了女孩，随后他被判犯有州虐待儿童罪。
2022 年 8 月，有人看到小手指伸进车门的缝隙后，向当局通报了这辆拖车的情况。当塞缪尔·贝特曼 (Samuel Bateman) 开车穿过弗拉格斯塔夫时，警方拦住了他的车，并发现车内有 3 名女孩，当时她们的年龄在 11 岁至 14 岁之间。拖车里有一个临时厕所、一张沙发和露营椅。
在这起联邦案件中，贝特曼因强迫年仅 9 岁的女孩与他和其他年轻人发生性行为，以及密谋绑架女孩而被判有罪，Netflix 剧集《相信我：假先知》重点讲述了这一故事。
这张未注明日期的照片由亚利桑那州科科尼诺县治安官办公室提供，照片中的塞缪尔·贝特曼是亚利桑那州和犹他州边境附近一个一夫多妻制小团体的领导人。（科科尼诺县治安官办公室，美联社，档案）
贝特曼此前声称拥有20多名“精神妻子”，其中包括10名18岁以下的女孩。他在州案件中为自己辩护作证，告诉陪审员他永远不会伤害他所爱的人。他在盘问中承认，他知道女孩们在炎热的拖车里呆了几个小时，而且通风不好，但他淡化了当时的情况。
“我只是相信自己是一名司机，”他说。“每次我们跳上那辆车时，我都祈求上帝保佑我。”
他声称当女孩们停下来时他以为他们已经出去了。他说，当他得知自己被拦下时他们还在车里时，他“非常震惊”。
在结案陈词中，检察官埃里克·鲁琴斯基 (Eric Ruchensky) 告诉陪审员，“常识是，在炎热的天气里，没有通风的情况下，不要用专为货运设计的拖车载人，这是常识。”
州案件的陪审员不应该听到联邦法院对贝特曼的定罪。法官禁止提供证据。但贝特曼在代表自己时多次提出这一点，导致法官从记录中删除了这些评论。
周五，陪审团在大约 40 分钟内做出了裁决，判定他犯有全部三项虐待儿童罪。。每项罪名都将被判处四年至八年的强制性刑期。法官有权连续或同时进行计数。量刑听证会定于 8 月 25 日举行。
美联社周五给贝特曼指定的顾问留下了语音邮件和电子邮件。
联邦当局表示，贝特曼自称为先知，他在建立耶稣基督后期圣徒原教旨主义教会的分支网络时，经常往返于亚利桑那州、犹他州、科罗拉多州和内布拉斯加州之间，该教会历来以亚利桑那州科罗拉多城和犹他州希尔代尔的邻近社区为基地。
他和他的追随者实行一夫多妻制，这是主流耶稣基督后期圣徒教会早期教义的遗产，该教会于 1890 年放弃了这种做法，现在严格禁止这种做法。
贝特曼是沃伦·杰夫斯值得信赖的追随者之一，沃伦·杰夫斯曾领导该教派，因性侵犯儿童而在德克萨斯州服无期徒刑。
随着时间的推移，一夫多妻教派的影响力在该教派历史上所在的城镇中显着减弱。2017年，法院命令将这些城镇置于监管之下，将教堂从政府和警察部门中排除出去。
但此后该地区发生了如此迅速的变化，以至于去年夏天他们被法院下令解除监管，这比预期早了近两年。据信，目前该教派的信徒只占该镇人口的一小部分。

item.title: 世界杯上的牛肉味十足，阿根廷球迷涌入德克萨斯州
item.description:
达拉斯（美联社）——让数千名阿根廷球迷前往德克萨斯州观看世界杯，争论是不可避免的。这与谁拥有最好的球队或莱昂内尔·梅西是否是锦标赛上最好的球员无关。这是关于谁生产出最好、最多汁的牛排，以及如何准备肉的问题。
没错：世界上两个顶级养牛地区之间存在着关于牛肉的争论，牛排在这些地区的饮食和文化中根深蒂固。根据美国农业部的数据，德克萨斯州的牛肉产量位居美国第一，在全球仅次于巴西。阿根廷排名第六。
这是一个棘手的问题：谁做得最好？
“阿根廷牛肉简直是无与伦比的。其美味的口感、切法的风格——无与伦比，”64 岁的阿根廷厨师卡洛斯·爱德华多·巴拉奥纳 (Carlos Eduardo Barahona) 说道，他自 1998 年以来一直住在德克萨斯州。
曾在阿根廷、乌拉圭和德克萨斯州的餐馆工作过的巴拉奥纳断言，从最便宜的肉到最贵的肉，阿根廷都是最好的。
“你可以用我们国家最便宜的切块制作（阿根廷）asado，你会喜欢它的。在这里，你可以使用最好的肉，比如里脊肉，根据其来源，它可能会变得坚硬、不可食用或变嫩。但我们的牛肉具有完全不同的风味特征，”巴拉奥纳说。
阿根廷肉牛大多在开阔的牧场上进行草饲，需要更长的时间才能到达上市状态。结果是肉质更瘦，带有浓郁的泥土风味。
2026 年 6 月 21 日星期日，达拉斯，阿根廷队的支持者马蒂亚斯·维德拉 (Matias Videla) 在世界杯 J 组足球比赛对阵奥地利队之前的集会上检查烤架上的肉。（美联社照片/胡里奥·科尔特斯）
德克萨斯州和美国的主要以谷物喂养的牛肉将有更多的大理石花纹——肌内脂肪的条纹，充当内部涂油剂，使肉多汁嫩滑——并且味道更甜。
“没有比美国牛肉更好的牛肉了，尤其是德克萨斯牛肉，”德克萨斯州农业专员席德·米勒 (Sid Miller) 说道。
但米勒说，阿根廷牛肉也非常好。感谢德克萨斯州。
米勒表示，他的机构十多年前开设了一个营销办事处，将德克萨斯州的养牛者与南美洲的牧场主联系起来，特别是阿根廷的牧场主。
“我不想贬低我们在阿根廷的朋友，但我们帮助他们进步了，”他说。
“他们缺乏遗传基因。我们确实让他们达到了相当高的质量。我们向他们出售了大量的精液、胚胎和种畜，”米勒补充道。
米勒祝贺阿根廷农民提高了奶牛的质量。
“他们的牛群有美国基因，所以它们应该很好，”米勒说。
阿根廷球迷贡萨洛·埃雷拉在观看梅西战胜奥地利队的两粒进球后，在德克萨斯州阿灵顿的一家沃尔玛浏览了包装肉。对于谁的牛肉更好的争论，他耸了耸肩。
“老实说，我没有看到太大的区别，”埃雷拉一边说，一边将四块丁骨牛排装进了购物车。
“关键是要准确地知道要购买哪些部位，并找到与我们在阿根廷吃的东西相当的东西，”他对 45 美元的价格摇摇头说道。
牛肉的玩笑很容易归结为菜谱以及对切块风格和厚度的偏好。毫不夸张地说，当涉及到调味料、烧烤、烟熏、黄油、胡椒、酱汁等时，这是一个口味问题。
达拉斯 Corrientes 348 阿根廷牛排馆的助理经理 Emmanuel Tobon 表示，牛排只用盐和豆科灌木木炭烹制而成。
“有很大的区别。德克萨斯人使用大量胡椒，他们使用黄油，他们使用一点烧烤（酱），”托邦说。“（阿根廷人）喜欢只用盐来呈现牛排的所有味道。”
周六，阿根廷在达拉斯至少还有一场比赛要打。世界杯期间，阿尔比塞莱斯特队的球迷们挤满了餐厅，希望能快速尝到家乡的味道。
“他们一直很享受德克萨斯州的文化，”托邦说。“（但是）很高兴拥有他们所有人，让他们有宾至如归的感觉。”
他说，阿根廷人对他们的牛排文化、代代相传的食谱以及大型家庭聚餐中烧烤师傅的“神圣”工作感到非常自豪。
对于来自布宜诺斯艾利斯、现居住在迈阿密附近的阿根廷人费尔南多·加西亚·莫里洛 (Fernando Garcia Morillo) 来说，两国的肉类都很棒。但每当他在美国点牛排时，他都渴望家乡的传统。
“我只点盐，不加胡椒，只是简单的，”莫里洛说。“有时他们会用很多酱汁。”
他否认美国和阿根廷之间有任何争执。
“也许我们会像往常一样与我们的邻国巴西展开竞争，”他说。“我喜欢美国的肉。”

item.title: 在伟大的美国州博览会上，您可以找到恐龙的肋骨。团结是另一回事
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——恐龙的肋骨。一座重 7,000 磅的沙堡，由泽西海岸的沙子制成。一个无人值守的摊位上有两把摇椅，还有一些空位。
该博览会于本周开幕，由 Freedom 250 主办，该组织是唐纳德·特朗普总统创建的，旨在举办其政府的官方活动。它的创建引起了与 America250 的紧张关系，America250 是一个国会创建的组织，旨在策划美国建国 250 周年纪念活动。国家广场上的活动将持续到七月。
周五早些时候，人群数量很少，在安检上花费的时间很少。入口通道直通 110 英尺高的摩天轮（博览会上唯一的游乐设施）和一个较小的拱门，类似于共和党总统用来装饰其他活动的拱门。
接受采访的人一致认为，这次展会是个好主意，但也许是错失了一个机会。
参加过集市的人都熟悉山羊、绵羊和小牛，它们的哞哞声在整个购物中心都能听到，这一切都是由来自西弗吉尼亚州杰斐逊县的美国华盛顿高中未来农民组织提供的。小鸡们的毛茸茸的柔软性闪闪发光，非常吸引人，并且有望与狂野的野马一起进行牛仔表演，以突显美国牛仔的崛起。
参观者们对各国聚集在一起并展示其独特的人物、著名的土著、工业、历史和地理多样性的热情团结在一起。他们还被对无党派庆祝美国团结的渴望所吸引。
“这里有友善的人、美好的活动、美好的家庭活动，”莎琳·博瓦特 (Sharyn Bovat) 说，她说她住在佛罗里达州，但自从今年心脏病发作后一直留在华盛顿地区。“这个国家的一半与另一半是分裂的。我希望他们能为所有人创造一个美国 250。我厌倦了政治。”
她说拱门是给这次活动增添政治色彩的主要因素。“这让我想起了德国，”她说。
展会展位的内部结构都类似于附近国家美术馆的新古典主义建筑，为参观者提供了美国风情的片段。
代表蒙大拿州的物品中有一个巨大的胸腔，是迷惑龙的放大版，是更知名的雷龙的近亲。
弗吉尼亚州的展位展示了该州在美国历史上的角色。哥伦比亚特区有一面巨大的横幅供游客签名，旁边还有一棵开满樱花的树。南卡罗来纳州邀请人们去享受果岭。
德克萨斯州包括一个太空舱、阿拉莫的正面和一座雕像——大德克萨斯州。外面排队的地方是亚利桑那州的展览，那里有一个互动装置，让游客可以将自己置身于该州的地形中。
参观新泽西州展位的参观者看到了一座华丽的沙堡，由一位艺术家用 7,000 磅从泽西海岸带来的沙子在四天内建造而成。
安迪·沃尔特斯 (Andy Walters) 和他的妻子克尔斯滕 (Kirsten) 带着来自俄亥俄州沃帕科内塔 (Wapakoneta) 的三个孩子来到这里，那里是阿波罗 11 号宇航员尼尔·阿姆斯特朗 (Neil Armstrong) 童年的家乡。“我认为这很好。我希望所有州都参与其中，”安迪·沃尔特斯说。“这有点令人沮丧，但这是一个好主意。”
克尔斯滕·沃尔特斯 (Kirsten Walters) 表示，她希望类似的活动能更频繁地举行，但不要有任何暗示。“我们很高兴来到这里并参与其中，”她说。
大多数州都参加了，将博览会几乎视为旅游机会。但没有充分的代表性。
缅因州、马萨诸塞州和宾夕法尼亚州以成本、日程安排或政治化问题为由，选择不派遣代表团。宾夕法尼亚州参议员约翰·费特曼（民主党人）和戴夫·麦考密克（共和党人）周六表示，他们已与几个商业贸易组织会面，“确保基斯通州将有一个值得展示其在我们国家历史上独特地位的展示。”
一些展位，包括夏威夷和阿拉斯加的展位，无人值守。夏威夷确实有几把摇椅，疲惫的游客可以充分利用它们。
来自纽约州罗马的小阿隆佐·刘易斯 (Alonzo Lewis Jr.) 和凯利·多米齐奥 (Kelly Domizio) 趁机减轻了负担。
“这真的很酷，”她说。“去过所有不同的州。”
“有必要吗，我不这么认为，”刘易斯说。“感觉很被迫。有太多的分离。”
多米齐奥说她还记得 1976 年的二百周年纪念。
她说，“有一种自豪感”和团结感。“我们很享受这一天，但感觉很被迫。”

item.title: 长期以来吸引游客的倒影池现在在特朗普的领导下受到了警方的审查
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——林肯纪念堂倒影池有很多意义。
浑浊。和平。臭。抗议、守夜、庆祝活动和令人惊叹的日出照片的背景。受到蚊虫和藻类的困扰。适合跑步的风景。首都精心设计的纪念性核心的关键部分。《阿甘正传》中标志性场景的拍摄地。
但有一件事通常不是：严格执行的警察区域。
进入泳池一直是非法的，但一般来说，涉水者最可能期望的是警察指示他离开。
但自从唐纳德·特朗普总统上周末在没有提供证据的情况下坚称破坏者对泳池内衬的损坏负有责任以来，这种情况发生了变化，在他指责前任总统忽视恶化后，破坏了他的翻修工作。本周提交的法庭文件显示，国家公园管理局向美国公园警察报告了 6 月 9 日发生的一起事件，其中一把尖刀或剃刀割破了泳池的新衬垫。
国民警卫队成员和公园警察在泳池周围的甲板上巡逻。美联社证实，一名男子在触摸已经剥落的油漆后被捕。他说他想检查一下新涂层，短暂地触摸了仍然附着的一块，然后在公园工作人员告诉他后不久就放开了。本周的某个时候，人们看到工作人员在该地区附近添加了围栏，政府将此归因于为七月四日庆祝活动做准备。
以下是多年来人们如何与倒影池互动的照片。
2016 年 5 月 31 日，捷克共和国布拉格的马丁·齐克 (Martin Zich) 在华盛顿国家广场的倒影池拍照后从水中涉水而出。（美联社照片/Andrew Harnik，档案）
仔细检查掩盖了这样一个事实：倒影池一直对游客具有吸引力。
1968 年 6 月 19 日，在华盛顿林肯纪念堂举行的团结日活动期间，许多穷人运动的支持者涌入倒影池。（美联社照片）
1968年的穷人运动期间，游泳池缓解了夏季的炎热。
在严寒的冬天，它变成了一个不太可能的城市溜冰场。
现在，这里是移动监视塔的所在地，执法人员徒步巡逻也有所增加，而纳米气泡的嗡嗡声则点缀着六月的空气。

item.title: 法官规定，与美国参议员丹·沙利文同名的男子有资格参加阿拉斯加州的初选
item.description:
阿拉斯加州朱诺（美联社）——法官周五裁定，与阿拉斯加州共和党参议员丹·沙利文同名、同党派的阿曼有资格在八月份的初选中挑战这位参议员。
高等法院法官托马斯·马修斯 (Thomas Matthews) 的裁决推翻了选举部门主任卡罗尔·比彻 (Carol Beecher) 6 月 15 日作出的取消挑战者资格并阻止其参加初选的决定。马修斯的裁决可以上诉至州最高法院。
阿拉斯加州共和党参议员丹·沙利文 (Dan Sullivan) 于 2025 年 6 月 30 日在华盛顿国会大厦向记者发表讲话。（美联社照片/Mark Schiefelbein）
该州律师表示，周二是最终裁决的最后期限，以便打印 8 月 18 日初选的选票。
法官裁定，该部门因丹·J·沙利文的候选资格并非“善意”而将其排除在外的决定并非基于宪法、阿拉斯加法律或该部门自己的规定。这位来自彼得堡小渔村的退休教师提出挑战现任教师。
法官写道：“相反，这一决定是基于一项新的、之前未曾声明过的‘善意’标准。”
美国州法律部发言人萨姆·柯蒂斯 (Sam Curtis) 周六在电子邮件中表示，该部门正在对这一决定提出上诉。丹·J·沙利文 (Dan J. Sullivan) 的律师杰弗里·罗宾逊 (Jeffrey Robinson) 在一封电子邮件中表示，他预计该部门会上诉，并且在阿拉斯加州最高法院对此案做出裁决之前无法发表评论。
关于两位丹·沙利文的争议凸显了现任总统连任竞选的利害关系。阿拉斯加竞选是预计秋季竞争激烈的大约六场美国参议院竞选之一，而该席位是民主党试图扭转的一个席位，以试图重新获得多数席位。
参议员和包括全国共和党参议院委员会在内的盟友谴责了挑战者参加竞选的努力，认为他的存在可能会让选民感到困惑。根据阿拉斯加州的选举制度，初选前四名候选人，无论党派如何，都将进入 11 月的大选。
这位参议员指责挑战者沙利文与民主党和民主党前美国众议员玛丽·佩尔托拉（被认为是参议员的主要对手）的竞选活动合作，造成混乱并增加佩尔托拉的机会。佩尔托拉的竞选团队和州民主党否认了这一指控，挑战者也否认了这一指控。
已申请竞选阿拉斯加美国参议员的丹·沙利文 (Dan Sullivan) 于 2026 年 6 月 26 日星期五在阿拉斯加州彼得堡摆姿势拍照。（美联社照片/凯蒂·霍姆伦德）
参议员沙利文和佩尔托拉是这场竞争激烈的竞选中最引人注目的候选人，也是唯一自称筹集到资金的候选人。
比彻表示，她确定挑战者沙利文没有资格参选，因为他的候选资格并不是出于善意，而是为了迷惑选民。她说，他已经以小丹尼尔·J·沙利文的身份登记投票，并在获得候选资格的同时，将自己的政党归属改为共和党。她还列举了他的竞选网站与参议员的网站之间的相似之处，以及他与一位顾问的合作，该顾问的客户包括一些民主党人。她没有提到发现任何所谓协调的证据。
在主张取消挑战者资格的过程中，该州律师反驳了这样的建议：投票的设计可以减少选民对两名同名、同党竞选同一职位的候选人的困惑。
阿拉斯加州法律部的律师雷切尔·威蒂（Rachel Witty）以及外部律师克里斯托弗·默里（Christopher Murray）和迈克尔·弗朗西斯科（Michael Francisco）在法庭文件中写道：“宪法并不要求各州在选票上放置虚假候选人，然后试图通过设计选择来减轻损害。”
挑战者沙利文的律师认为，宪法规定了参议院的三项专有资格，仅涉及年龄、公民身份和居住权。他们表示，比彻没有法律权力将他们的客户从选票中剔除。
挑战者沙利文表示，与现任总统分享姓名和党派关系给了他“一个即时扩音器”。但这位 69 岁的退休教师和前美国林务局雇员表示，他考虑参选已经有一段时间了，并对这位参议员感到越来越沮丧。
他最初在该州的候选人名单上被认证为丹·J·沙利文（Dan J. Sullivan），参议员被列为丹·S·沙利文（Dan S. Sullivan），并被确定为现任参议员。
这个故事已经更新，以更正丹·J·沙利文律师的姓氏：是罗宾逊，而不是罗伯逊。

item.title: 佛得角成世界杯淘汰赛最小国家
item.description:
休斯顿（美联社）——小小佛得角凭借40岁门将沃齐尼亚的出色发挥，克服重重困难，成为进入世界杯淘汰赛的最小国家，沃齐尼亚体现了国家的勇气。
“我们很小，”他说。“但我们有宽广的胸怀，我们是战士。”
佛得角在小组赛中以不可思议的成绩完成了连续第三次世界杯平局，周五晚上0-0战平沙特阿拉伯，晋级世界杯。
这个非洲西海岸的小岛国首次登上足球最盛大的舞台，已0-0战平2010年冠军西班牙，随后又以2-2战胜乌拉圭。
“球队非常渴望向全世界展示这一点，”佛得角教练布比斯塔披着佛得角国旗说道。“我们为达到这个阶段感到自豪。我们已经表明，我们是一个小国家，但我们为我们想要实现的目标而奋斗。”
佛得角全取三分，排名第二，落后西班牙队，西班牙队在周五晚上击败乌拉圭队，获得小组第一。
佛得角将于7月3日在迈阿密迎战世界杯卫冕冠军阿根廷队。
三场小组赛全部打平并不能保证在大型足球赛事中晋级。但过去也有几支球队做到过，包括1958年的威尔士、1990年的爱尔兰和荷兰、1998年的智利。不过，新西兰也在2010年世界杯上三场平局而被淘汰。
比赛前夕，布比斯塔沉思道：“每个人都有权梦想，没有什么是不可能的。”
蓝鲨队证明了他的正确性，他们克服了看似不可逾越的困难，让这个人口仅超过 50 万的国家进入了 32 强。
一名脸上画着群岛旗帜的妇女举着一块牌子，上面写着：“小岛屿，大梦想”。
这些弱者在世界舞台上继续他们的迷人之旅时，已经实现了他们的梦想。
他们通过沃齐尼亚 (Vozinha) 的另一场精彩比赛做到了这一点，沃齐尼亚 (Vozinha) 锦标赛的成功帮助他在 Instagram 上积累了超过 1600 万粉丝。
他在上半场补时阶段做出了一次扑救，接住了穆罕默德·卡诺的头球，让沙特阿拉伯队没有得分。第 66 分钟，他再次扑出穆罕默德·阿布·沙马特的射门。
第三次进球发生在第 92 分钟，他挡住了阿卜杜拉·哈姆丹 (Abdullah Al-Hamdan) 的射门。
“我们的国家队有很多优秀的人才，”沃齐尼亚说。“也许对你们中的许多人来说，你们认为佛得角球员不够好。但我们来到这里是为了证明我们有很强的实力，我们来这里是为了竞争，我们的球员可以在大赛、大联赛中的任何地方踢球。”
人群中一群赤裸上身的男子每人在胸前画了一个他的名字字母，为佛得角欢呼。
但沃齐尼亚在 68,278 名阿希斯母亲安娜·坎迪达·埃沃拉 (Ana Candida Evora) 的豪华套房中拥有更大的粉丝，她挥舞着一面小小的佛得角国旗。这是她在本届世界杯上的第二场比赛，此前沃齐尼亚因签证问题错过了对阵西班牙队的史诗般的七次扑救表现。
第50分钟，佛得角有机会得分，但凯文·皮纳的远射稍稍高出横梁。第74分钟，拉罗斯·杜阿尔特(Laros Duarte)在禁区中央的射门被门将穆罕默德·奥瓦伊斯(Mohammed Al-Owais)挡出，再次获得机会。
最后几秒，努诺·达科斯塔从禁区中路左侧射门，这是最后的得分机会。
但这并不重要，因为终场哨响几分钟后，西班牙队就完成了对乌拉圭队的胜利，引发了佛得角球员和球迷的欢乐庆祝，不少人喜极而泣。
在带领球队达到新的高度后，布比斯塔被问到他是否能想象在参加锦标赛时能有这样的表现。
“我一直说佛得角迟早会登上这样的舞台，”他说。“当然很难有这样的预测，但我一直都知道。”
沙特阿拉伯队在小组赛中以两分的成绩被淘汰。
“我们在创造事物、控制比赛和创造行动方面非常差，”教练乔治斯·多尼斯说。“这样就无法赢得比赛。这会非常困难。”

item.title: 埃及队1-1战平伊朗队，晋级世界杯小组赛
item.description:
西雅图（美联社）——一名伊朗球迷冲向球场庆祝，七名保安才成功将他制服。
周五晚上，在第 93 分钟，绍贾·哈利勒扎德 (Shoja Khalilzadeh) 射门越过埃及守门员穆斯塔法·肖贝尔 (Mostafa Shobeir)，伊朗队似乎以 2-1 领先，随后他被整个伊朗队围攻。
在一些欢乐的时刻，伊朗确信自己在今年世界杯扩大到的 48 支球队中首次晋级世界杯淘汰赛。
然而，最后时刻的致胜进球因越位而被取消，埃及队顺利晋级小组赛。
与此同时，在两队战成1-1平后，伊朗队将不得不等待一天才能知道自己的命运。埃及队将在32强赛中迎战澳大利亚队。
伊朗队主教练阿米尔·加勒诺伊对视频回放中球队的第三个进球表示遗憾。
“技术就是正义，”加勒诺伊用波斯语说道，“但是，我对我们的厄运感到不安。”
在西雅图举行的这场伊朗和埃及都不想参加的“骄傲赛”结束后，法老队在 G 组获得第二名。比利时队于 6 月 15 日与埃及队 1-1 战平，在不列颠哥伦比亚省温哥华以 5-1 击败新西兰队，获得小组冠军。
埃及队主教练霍萨姆·哈桑很庆幸他的球队没有滑落到小组第三，如果哈利勒扎德的进球成功的话，情况就会如此。
“我们知道我们已经晋级了，我们在小组中名列前茅，”霍桑说。“我感谢上帝所做的一切。这个进球被判越位后，我非常高兴。”
与此同时，伊朗队仍有可能在今年世界杯扩大到的 48 支球队中首次晋级淘汰赛。但是，周五的比赛之后，他们不再掌控自己的命运。
“我们来这里也是为了创造我们的历史，”中场球员鲁兹贝·切什米说道。“（周六），如果我们的梦想成真，感谢上帝。如果没有，我们为我们的比赛和我们的球员感到自豪，因为我们的比赛表现。过去三场比赛，我们坚持了下来，所以让我们看看会发生什么。”
埃及队在第五分钟凭借马哈茂德·萨贝尔的进球早早取得领先。前利物浦球星穆罕默德·萨拉赫在禁区内表现出色，随后萨贝尔在伊朗门将阿里雷扎·贝兰万德的两腿之间射门。
第11分钟，埃及门将穆斯塔法·肖贝尔扑出点球，伊朗队扳平比分，但第14分钟拉明·雷扎伊安的进球将比分追平。
伊朗队稍稍落后之后，其球员们对场外的诸多麻烦表达了不满。鉴于伊朗战争，该团队受到了美国国土安全部实施的旅行限制。
今年三月，伊朗寻求将小组赛移至与其有外交关系的墨西哥举行。其将大本营从亚利桑那州图森迁至蒂华纳的请求在球队抵达前两周获得批准。
加勒诺伊表示，团队成员仅限于酒店和训练设施内，没有以任何身份探索蒂华纳。几名球队官员和后勤人员被禁止随球队前往美国。
“我们没有康复，这里没有任何后勤人员来帮助我们。我们总是抱怨这些事情，但没有人帮助我们——没有人，”鲁兹贝·切什米说。“如你所知，恢复是一件重要的事情。小细节会影响足球。”
在洛杉矶附近的前两场比赛中，球队直到前一天才被允许出行，并且必须在每场比赛结束后立即返回墨西哥。美国然后放松了限制，允许球队在周五比赛前两天前往西雅图。
加勒诺伊表示，周五比赛结束后，球队再次必须立即飞回蒂华纳。
“我们受到了非常非常恶劣的待遇，”加勒诺伊说。“我希望世界能够意识到这些问题。”
不过，即使只是短暂的一段时间，在哈利勒扎德险些进球后，伊朗的蔑视也随之而来。伊朗队并没有以童话般的结局结束周五的比赛，但他们在比赛中仍然充满活力——至少还能再坚持 24 小时。
“这些年轻的伊朗国家队球员所做的事情应该被载入史册，”加勒诺伊说。“为什么？因为主人用最糟糕的方式对待我们。”
美联社自由撰稿人马克·莫斯切蒂为本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 乌拉圭门将费尔南多·穆斯莱拉在对阵西班牙的比赛中出现失误后，中场休息时应他的要求被替换下场
item.description:
墨西哥瓜达拉哈拉（美联社）——主教练马塞洛·贝尔萨表示，乌拉圭门将费尔南多·穆斯莱拉在周五晚上对阵西班牙的世界杯比赛中场休息时因失误导致进球，要求被换下。
下半场比赛开始，塞尔吉奥·罗切特上场，乌拉圭0-1落后，濒临淘汰。结果依然成立。
“他是决定离开的人，”贝尔萨谈到穆斯莱拉时说道，穆斯莱拉在球队2-2战平佛得角以及首场1-1战平沙特阿拉伯的比赛中也出现过失误。
第 42 分钟，穆斯莱拉未能挡住阿莱克斯·巴埃纳禁区内的射门。他似乎被弹跳愚弄了，无法用足够坚定的双手接球以将射门完全偏转远离球网。
40岁的穆斯莱拉几年前退出了国际比赛，今年早些时候在贝尔萨的带领下重返国家队，贝尔萨的决定遭到了许多乌拉圭球迷的批评。
“我的决定是经过深思熟虑的。我做了所有我认为必要的评估，”贝尔萨说。“现在，当然，我不能否认发生的事情并评价他的表现。但他是一名门将，今年表现出色，也是一名具有出色个性和品格的球员。”
在周日与佛得角的比赛中，穆斯莱拉在马蒂亚斯·奥利维拉的一次糟糕传球后失位，导致扳平比分的空门进球。而对阵沙特的比赛中，他头球攻门后未能保住皮球，沙特队则反弹得分。
美联社撰稿人玛丽亚·韦尔扎为本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯要在麦迪逊广场花园结婚吗？我们所知道的
item.description:
AreTaylor Swift and Travis Kelcereally getting married at Madison Square Garden?
Frenzied speculation surrounding the superstar singer and football player’s upcoming wedding has spiked over the past few days as reports swirl that the two are getting married the first week in July at one of New York’s iconic landmarks.
Yet nearly a year after Kelce and Swift announcedtheir engagementwith the caption “Your English teacher and your gym teacher are getting married,” they have disclosed little about their plans.
Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce, right, is congratulated by Taylor Swift as they celebrate the Chiefs’ victory over the Buffalo Bills in the AFC Championship NFL football game, Jan. 26, 2025, in Kansas City, Mo. (AP Photo/Charlie Riedel, File)
Nothing has been publicly confirmed by the couple, despite The Associated Press’ multiple requests to Swift’s representative for comment.
Citing an entertainment industry executive and another person with knowledge of the matter,The New York Timesreported that the couple were planning a gathering of 100 people at Madison Square Garden — an arena that seats up to 19,500 people — on July 2 followed by a second event at the arena for about 1,000 guests on July 3.
The Associated Press has been unable to independently confirm those details, but there are no public events planned at the Garden from June 29 until a Bon Jovi concert on July 7.
Public records show that the city issued a permit for loading and unloading theatrical materials at the arena from June 29 to July 4. Winick Productions, a company that has produced red carpet events for the Grammy and Tony award shows and movie premieres, also applied for a permit to set up a canopy or tent outside the Garden for an event involving up to 999 people.
Meanwhile, just a few weeks prior, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani jokingly referenced the reports of Swift and Kelce’s wedding during apress conference. Mamdani was answering questions about safety during the World Cup when he said, “We know it coincides with July Fourth, America 250, Taylor Swift’s wedding all happening at the same time, and we are so excited to welcome the world here.”
Mamdani, however, said he was not invited to the wedding.
“I wish them a lovely wedding. I’ll listen to ‘Only the Young’ at home on my own,” he said, referencing one of Swift’s songs.
New York City police on horseback patrol the street by Madison Square Garden during Game 5 of the NBA Finals basketball series against the San Antonio Spurs, June 13, 2026, in New York. (AP Photo/Heather Khalifa, File)
Madison Square Garden may not scream “bridal,” but the venue is available for private rentals, advertising a banquet capacity for 1,250 — or 2,000 if you are only serving cocktails. And it has hosted weddings before. Sly Stone got married to Kathy Silva there in 1974 before thousands of fans. And more than 2,000 couples were wed in a mass ceremony at the Garden officiated bythe Rev. Sun Myung Moonin 1982.
Located above Penn Station, the busiest rail hub in the U.S., the Garden doesn’t scream “privacy” either.
But it does have guarded entrances, a secure garage and a lack of windows, which would allow Kelce, Swift and celebrity guests to stay out of sight of photographers or camera-equipped drones.
That need for privacy popped up earlier in June when a large tent appeared next door to Swift’s Watch Hill estate in Rhode Island. Despite organizers denying the event was for Swift, a wave of speculation bubbled up online as photographers and some Swifties headed to the town to see if they couldcatch a glimpse of a possible wedding.
Yet others have theorized that the MSG buzz could be an elaborate smoke screen to throw off attention to the couple’s real wedding plans. Swift did once write, “No, you can’t come to the wedding,” in her song “But Daddy I Love Him,” which some fans have been reupping lately as a reminder that the wedding isn’t supposed to be a public spectacle.
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce watch the second half of Game 3 in the Eastern Conference finals NBA basketball playoffs series between the New York Knicks and the Cleveland Cavaliers in Cleveland, May 23, 2026. (AP Photo/Tim Phillis, File)
Swift joked in October that “anyone I’ve ever talked to” would be invited to the wedding, telling Graham Norton that she believed “the only stressful weddings” are those that are small and force people to make aggressive cuts to the guest list.
Yet just who exactly will show up is to be determined. Aside from family, Kelce’s Kansas City Chiefs teammate Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, will likely be in attendance. For Swift, close friends like Selena Gomez, Abigail Anderson Berard,the Haim sisters,Emma Stone and Gigi Hadid will all likely attend.
Chiefs coach Andy Reid said he couldn’t talk about it when asked by The Associated Press if he was going to the wedding.
“If it’s like when I got married, my wife did everything, so I just kind of followed her lead on it, showed up, right? Maybe he’s doing more but he looks like he’s pretty focused in on this job here, too,” Reid said in early June.
Perhaps another clue why the week of the Fourth of July makes sense forSwift and Kelce’s weddingis that the popstar has long been known for throwing elaborate parties over the American holiday.
It wasn’t too long ago that fans dubbed her Fourth of July events as “Taymerica,” where celebrities showed up at her Rhode Island estate wearing red, white and blue swimsuits, waving American flags and eventually shared some social media photos with the public.
The timing also works with Kelce’s football schedule, given the tight end once joked on his “New Heights” podcast, “Don’t make my friends have to choose whether or not they have to sell their tickets that week.”
Associated Press writers Anthony Izaguirre and Maria Sherman in New York and David Skretta in Kansas City, Missouri, contributed reporting.

item.title: 新星座乐队将携首张专辑登陆科罗纳首都
item.description:
墨西哥城（美联社）——波特兰独立流行二人组 New Constellations 希望在墨西哥城的 Corona Capital 音乐节上留下自己的印记，他们将在 11 月带着首张专辑《It Comes in Waves》抵达墨西哥城，此前他们已经活跃了近十年，并以朋友的身份彼此了解。
这一切都始于一则寻找音乐家的广告，这则广告是由歌手 Harlee Case 在她的 Instagram 帐户上发布的。多乐器演奏家乔什·史密斯（Josh Smith）从十几岁起就认识她，接听了电话。
“我看到它并想，‘哦，那应该是我。’所以我联系了她并说，‘嘿，我将成为你乐队的一员，’”史密斯说。
凯斯的声音非常有力，正如他的热门歌曲《热血》以及《It Comes in Waves》中的其他歌曲，如《I'm Waiting Now》和《Dandelion》。她在她的家里长大，周围都是音乐。
“我妈妈也是一名歌手，但我没有接受过任何训练，我只是真的很关心唱歌，我一生都在努力，你知道，提高，总是努力提高，”他说。
就史密斯而言，他是家里的第一位音乐家，从十几岁起就开始学习艺术，凯斯也加入了一个青年团体。他们都住在波特兰，他们认为这座城市是艺术的沃土。
《Hot Blooded》是他们的第一首单曲，陪伴了他们五年，在《It Comes in Waves》中，它与专为专辑制作的最新歌曲以及其他老歌混合在一起。对于乐队来说，这是第一阶段的结束，其亲密的歌词与复古合成器和另类电子音乐搭配在一起。
“我想我想称我们为 Dream Pop，这就是我最近一直在说的，情感化的、个人化的音乐，”史密斯说。“如果你要听这张唱片，就要准备好变得个性化。”
这部作品是凯斯和史密斯的合作作品，通常以她的一种自由风格开始，然后他们共同发展它。
“昨晚我第一次拿出吉他，开始创作一首歌，”凯斯说。“但我并没有真正坐在桌子旁讨论我自己写的东西。”
他们音乐的另一个要点是他们喜欢与公众一起测试他们的歌曲。
“我们正在研究写歌、演奏它们，然后弄清楚如何在巡演中改进它们，然后录制它们，与为专辑创作新歌然后演奏它们之间的区别，”凯斯说。
这正是他们想要在 Corona Capital 做的事情，为此，他们是确认参加将于 11 月 20 日至 22 日在墨西哥城 Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez 举行的音乐节的乐队之一，与 Gorillaz、The Strokes、Twenty One Pilots、The XX 和 Underworld 等艺术家一起参加。
“我们太兴奋了！”史密斯说。“多年来我一直听说这件事，我们有朋友玩过，我们认识的每个去过或玩过的人都简单地说这是有史以来最好的事情。”
在他们的音乐会上，他们将由曾一起巡演美国的其他音乐家陪同，因此这将是一个更接近乐队而不是二重奏的配置。
当凯斯和史密斯不创作音乐时，他们的兴趣截然不同。
“我真的很喜欢热瑜伽，我喜欢它，”歌手说。“我真的很喜欢冥想和呆在森林里，我家后面就有一片森林，我花了很多时间在里面，你知道，尝试与树木和类似的东西交谈。”
相反，史密斯喜欢打飞盘高尔夫或躺在沙发上玩电子游戏。

item.title: Saks 正式摆脱美国破产法第 11 章破产保护，债务减少并更名
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——内曼·马库斯 (Neiman Marcus)、萨克斯第五大道 (Saks Fifth Avenue) 和波道夫·古德曼 (Bergdorf Goodman) 的母公司萨克斯全球 (Saks Global) 于周五正式摆脱美国破产法第 11 章破产保护，门店数量减少，债务减少，采取了更加专注于迎合富人的战略，并更换了新名称。
该公司周五表示，新实体将被称为 Exemplar Luxury Group，并改善资产负债表，包括减少近 75% 的债务和 5 亿美元的额外融资。其首席执行官 Geoffroy van Raemdonck 表示，这家总部位于纽约的公司在经历了几年的动荡之后，已准备好迎接新的篇章。
范雷姆东克周五在接受美联社电话采访时表示：“对于该组织来说，今天确实是全新的一天，这三个标志性旗帜拥有合适的资金、合适的股权和光明的未来。”
范·雷姆东克 (Van Raemdonck) 表示，新名称表明该公司致力于打造堪称典范的购物体验，即在销售人员的帮助和客户数据宝库的帮助下，为客户提供最好的商品和更好的个性化服务。他说，该公司雇用了 1,500 多名销售人员，每人销售的商品超过 100 万美元。
Saks Global 因竞争加剧以及 2024 年 7 月收购奢侈品行业竞争对手 Neiman Marcus 时背负巨额债务而受到冲击，于今年 1 月申请破产保护。
据该公司称，破产前，该公司拥有 33 家 Saks 商店和 36 家 Neiman Marcus 商店，以及位于第五大道的 Bergdorf Goodman 商店和大约 70 家 Saks Off 5th 折扣店。
现在，共有 49 家商店——15 家 Saks Fifth Avenue 商店、33 家 Neiman Marcus 商店和 Bergdorf Goodman 商店。该公司表示，该公司关闭了大部分 Saks Off Fifth 折扣店，目前拥有 12 家门店。
Exemplar Luxury Group 表示，在整个重组过程中，它一直与 Pentwater Capital Management 和 Bracebridge Capital 合作。两家公司将在七人董事会中派出两名代表。此外，该公司表示，van Raemdonck 以及 Ulta Beauty 前首席执行官 Dave Kimbell 和最近担任酩悦轩尼诗全球首席执行官的 Philippe Schaus 将担任董事会成员。
